fundamentals.name: Applied Optoelectronics, Inc.
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: Applied Optoelectronics, Inc. engages in the design, manufacture, and sale of fiber-optic networking products in the United States, Taiwan, and China. The company offers optical modules, optical filters, lasers, laser components, subassemblies, transmitters and transceivers, turn-key equipment, headend, node, and distribution equipment, as well as amplifiers. It sells its products to internet data center operators, cable television, telecom equipment manufacturers, fiber-to-the-home, and internet service providers through its direct and indirect sales channels. Applied Optoelectronics, Inc. was incorporated in 1997 and is headquartered in Sugar Land, Texas.

judgment.summary: Rapidly growing company with extremely high analyst growth estimates, but currently unprofitable with a high forward P/E.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter missed EPS estimates (-0.07 vs -0.05); next earnings date is 2026-08-06.
positives: Revenue growth 51.4% YoY — indicating strong top-line expansion.
Analyst growth est, current Q: 109.4% and current FY: 441.7% — exceptional projected growth.
Forward P/E 33.52 is lower than trailing P/E of n/a, suggesting expected future earnings growth.
PEG ratio 0.78 — suggests growth may justify the current valuation.
Current ratio 3.83 and Quick ratio 2.85 — strong short-term liquidity.
negatives: Operating margin -8.6% and Profit margin -8.5% — the company is currently losing money.
Return on equity -6.1% and Return on assets -3.3% — poor profitability metrics.
Free cash flow (TTM): $-445.58M — significant cash burn.
Last reported EPS was a miss (-0.07 vs -0.05 estimate).
Debt/Equity: 25.36 — high leverage relative to equity.

news.paragraph: Applied Optoelectronics (AAOI) is experiencing significant demand, with reports indicating Nvidia is seeking 20 times more supply, contributing to a massive stock surge. Despite a recent earnings miss and negative margins, the company's revenue growth is strong, and analyst estimates for the current quarter and fiscal year are exceptionally high. The narrative appears to be shifting towards a supply bottleneck driving substantial growth, though concerns about valuation and profitability persist.
